hypothesis: If universal machine-learning interatomic potentials trained on broad, non-MOF datasets (augmented with dispersion corrections) are applied to metal–organic frameworks without fine-tuning, then they will reproduce DFT reference geometries, NpT stability at 300 K/1 bar, bulk moduli, and 300 K heat capacities across diverse frameworks in close agreement, because their learned, symmetry-aware representations capture transferable interatomic interactions across metal–ligand and organic bonding environments.
